source.description: A corrected six-panel pencil process contact sheet reviewed for suspension geometry and honest occlusion order.
source.notes: One consistent near-front porch swing on warm drawing paper. A light cushion footprint is reserved before the visible wooden frame is built, so no finished seat boards or slats are drawn and then hidden. Both armrests and their eye bolts are established before two vertical chains are added directly above them.

process_strategy: The contact sheet begins with the outer bench envelope, back guide, and a light rounded cushion footprint. The next panel builds only wood that will remain visible around that footprint: the front apron, narrow exposed side rails, armrests, supports, and eye bolts. It must not draw seat boards, seat slats, seams, or wood grain inside the reserved cushion area. Panel 3 adds one vertical chain directly above each eye bolt. Panel 4 commits the cushion silhouette and adds the back frame with visible vertical back slats. Later panels add restrained color and final graphite cleanup without revealing hidden seat construction or rerouting either chain.

final_elements.name: bench envelope, back guide, and cushion footprint
final_elements.description: A light near-front bench envelope, tall back guide, and rounded rectangular footprint reserving the area the cushion will occupy.

final_elements.name: visible wooden frame, armrests, supports, and eye bolts
final_elements.description: The front apron, narrow exposed rails, two armrests with front supports, and one small eye bolt on each armrest; no detailed seat surface is drawn inside the reserved cushion footprint.

final_elements.name: two chains outside the woodwork
final_elements.description: Two linked chains descend through blank space outside the bench and end cleanly at the established armrest eye bolts without continuing below or passing through wood.

final_elements.name: seat cushion and visible slatted back frame
final_elements.description: A soft cushion committed over its reserved footprint plus the tall back frame and evenly spaced vertical slats that remain visible behind it.

final_elements.name: pale teal cushion tint, wood tone, and floor shadow
final_elements.description: Restrained color on established forms with a soft shadow below.

frames.visible_job: Places the outer bench envelope, tall back guide, and light rounded cushion footprint in a balanced near-front view.
introduces: bench envelope, back guide, and cushion footprint

frames.visible_job: Builds the front apron, narrow exposed rails, both armrests and supports, and one eye bolt per armrest while leaving the reserved cushion area free of finished seat detail.
introduces: visible wooden frame, armrests, supports, and eye bolts
requires_prior_elements: bench envelope, back guide, and cushion footprint

frames.visible_job: Adds two linked chains through blank space outside the bench, each ending at an established armrest eye bolt with no chain below the mount.
requires_prior_elements: visible wooden frame, armrests, supports, and eye bolts

frames.step_name: Add the cushion and back slats
frames.visible_job: Commits the cushion over its reserved footprint and adds the tall back frame with separated vertical slats, without covering any previously finished seat detail.
introduces: seat cushion and visible slatted back frame
requires_prior_elements: bench envelope, back guide, and cushion footprint
visible wooden frame, armrests, supports, and eye bolts

frames.visible_job: Adds pale color and a soft floor shadow to established shapes without moving either chain or eye bolt.
introduces: pale teal cushion tint, wood tone, and floor shadow
requires_prior_elements: visible wooden frame, armrests, supports, and eye bolts
seat cushion and visible slatted back frame

final_step.visible_job: Only strengthens established contours, outside chain paths, eye-bolt connections, visible back slats, cushion color, exposed wood tone, and shadow.

rejection_checks: No chain, eye bolt, armrest, support, visible back slat, cushion, tint, or shadow may first appear in the final image.
The swing must retain one near-front angle, scale, and chain placement across all frames.
Panel 2 must contain both armrests and both eye bolts but no chains; panel 3 must add exactly two chains to those established eye bolts.
Both chains must stay outside the armrests and bench silhouette, end at the visible armrest eye bolts, and never continue below or pass through a wooden part.
The cushion footprint must be reserved in panel 1; no later panel may draw seat boards, seat slats, seams, or wood grain inside that footprint.
Every dark structural line added before the cushion must remain visible in the finished panel; construction guides may be erased, but finished detail may not be hidden by the cushion.
The only slats in the lesson are the vertical back slats that remain visible above and behind the cushion.
No late panel may add porch architecture, plants, pillows, or decorative carving.
